statement: The system shall provide a baseline configuration and allow creation of variant configurations with longer arms, higher lift capacity, or waterproofing as required by a project.
The system shall enter a safe mode when a movement exceeds the system's programmed capabilities, such as moving too fast.
The system shall enforce hard and soft stops in both software and hardware.
The system shall display the range of each joint in the UI and change its colour when the joint approaches its limits.
The system shall allow tasks to have a configurable time limit with an additional 50% buffer.
The system shall provide a complete simulation that models the entire scene exactly.
The system shall support teach files that allow programming a target point for autonomous movement.
The system shall allow programming of repeatable tool change positions and provide a home button to move the arm to predefined states.
The system shall enable handover of control to the operator after autonomous movement reaches a near target.
The system shall provide fixed camera views, such as a goal line camera, that remain constant during tasks.
The system shall support zoom functionality, allowing operators to zoom out for overview and zoom in as they approach task areas.
The system shall provide an augmented reality overlay that guides operators on where to focus or align during tasks.
The system shall prioritize high image resolution over frame rate.
The system shall provide force feedback to indicate when motion should stop.
The system shall provide a ghost mode that visualizes the predicted end position of a commanded movement before execution.
The system shall define no-go zones and provide audio and haptic alerts when the operator approaches them.
The system shall use computer vision to recognize material types and incorporate this information into no‑go zone enforcement.
The system shall detect overload on the gripper, indicate when the fingers are opening, and prevent dropping objects.
